preamble: The shift from the near-open front vowel [æ] toward the high-front vowel [i] is an extreme form of vowel raising and tensing. In English dialects, this rarely results in a pure [i] but frequently reaches [ɪə] or [iə], particularly in stressed environments.

examples.note: Extreme raising and tensing of the TRAP vowel, often characterized as sounding like 'kee-at'.

sources: Labov, W., Ash, S., & Boberg, C. (2006). The Atlas of North American English. https://www.degruyterbrill.com/document/doi/10.1515/9783110167467/html?lang=en
